input.prompt:
Explore the unsloth codebase structure. I need to understand:
1. The overall directory layout - what folders and key files exist
2. Where save/export methods are defined (look for methods like save_model, save_pretrained, push_to_hub, export_to_gguf, export_to_ollama, etc.)
3. How LoRA adapter weights (A and B matrices) are stored/accessed
4. Any existing ONNX-related code

Please thoroughly search for:
- All files containing "save" or "export" in their names
- The main classes that models inherit from
- How `FastLanguageModel` or similar classes are structured
- Where LoRA-specific code lives (look for lora_A, lora_B, peft, LoraLayer, etc.)
- Any existing serialization patterns

Provide file paths and key code snippets for all findings. Be thorough - read the actual file contents for the most important files.

245

260	    pass
261	
262	    # First check for a token!

265	        try:
266	            username = whoami(token = token)["name"]

268	            raise RuntimeError(
269	                "Unsloth: Please supply a token!\n"\

271	            )
272	        pass
273	    pass
274	
275	    assert(maximum_memory_usage > 0 and maximum_memory_usage <= 0.95)
276	
277	    # Clean memory up first
278	    for _ in range(3):
279	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
280	        gc.collect()
281	    pass
282	
283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")

1324	    tokenizer            = None,
1325	    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit", # ["lora", "merged_16bit", "merged_4bit"]

1337	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,

1339	    """
1340	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1341	        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.
1342	
1343	        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
1344	        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
1345	        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
1346	        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
1347	    """
1348	    if tokenizer is None:
1349	        logger.warning_once(
1350	            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
1351	            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
1352	        )
1353	    pass
1354	
1355	    arguments = dict(locals())
1356	    arguments["model"] = self
1357	    del arguments["self"]
1358	    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1359	    for _ in range(3):
1360	        gc.collect()
1361	pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias

283	    save_method = save_method.lower().replace(" ", "_")

285	        raise RuntimeError(
286	            "Unsloth: You must select one of 3 options when saving models:\n"\

1951	def unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf(
1952	    self,
1953	    repo_id              : str,
1954	    tokenizer            = None,
1955	    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
1956	    first_conversion     : str = None,

1964	    revision             : str = None,
1965	    commit_description   : str = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster",
1966	    tags                 : Optional[List[str]] = None,
1967	    temporary_location   : str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
1968	    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.85,
1969	):
1970	    """
1971	        Same as .push_to_hub(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1972	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1973	
1974	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1975	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1976	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1977	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1978	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1979	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1980	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1981	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1982	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1983	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1984	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1985	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1986	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1987	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1988	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1989	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1990	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1991	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1992	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1993	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1994	    """
1995	    if tokenizer is None:
1996	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1997	
1998	    arguments = dict(locals())
1999	    arguments["model"]          = self
2000	    arguments["tokenizer"]      = tokenizer
2001	    arguments["save_directory"] = repo_id
2002	    arguments["push_to_hub"]    = False # We save ourselves
2003	    arguments["save_method"]   = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2004	    del arguments["self"]
2005	    del arguments["repo_id"]
2006	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
2007	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
2008	
2009	    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
2010	    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)
2011	
2012	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
2013	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
2014	
2015	        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
2016	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
2017	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
2018	            python_install.wait()
2019	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
2020	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2021	            makefile = None
2022	        else:
2023	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
2024	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
2025	            git_clone.wait()
2026	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
2027	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2028	            python_install.wait()
2029	        pass
2030	    else:
2031	        try:
2032	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
2033	            makefile = None
2034	        except:
2035	            # Retry by recloning llama.cpp

2569	def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
2570	    import inspect
2571	    import types
2572	    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List
2573	
2574	    # And now re add our saving methods!
2575	    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2576	        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
2577	    else:
2578	        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
2579	    pass
2580	
2581	    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
2582	    signature = signature[1:]
2583	    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
2584	    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")
2585	
2586	    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
2587	    """
2588	    {docs}
2589	    """
2590	    arguments = dict(locals())
2591	    del arguments["self"]
2592	    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
2593	        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
2594	        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
2595	    elif "tags" in arguments:
2596	        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
2597	    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
2598	        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2599	
2600	    if "commit_message" in arguments:
2601	        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
2602	        if commit_message is not None:
2603	            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
2604	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
2605	                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
2606	        else:
2607	            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
2608	        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message
2609	
2610	    if "commit_description" in arguments:
2611	        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
2612	        if commit_description is not None:
2613	            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
2614	            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
2615	                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
2616	        else:
2617	            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
2618	        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description
2619	
2620	    # Update model tag
2621	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2622	        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
2623	            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
2624	            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
2625	            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
2626	        )
2627	    pass
2628	
2629	    try:
2630	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2631	    except:
2632	        del arguments["tags"]
2633	        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
2634	    pass
2635	
2636	    if hasattr(self, "config"):
2637	        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
2638	    pass
2639	    '''
2640	    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())
2641	
2642	    original_model = model
2643	    while True:
2644	
2645	        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
2646	            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
2647	            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
2648	            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
2649	                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
2650	            pass
2651	        pass
2652	
2653	        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
2654	        else: break
2655	    pass
2656	
2657	    # Add saving methods to top level model

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)

## Comprehensive Unsloth Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the unsloth codebase. Here's what I found:

### 1. OVERALL DIRECTORY LAYOUT

**Root Structure:**
```

│   ├── tokenizer_utils.py     # Tokenizer utilities

│   │   ├── cross_entropy_loss.py
│   │   ├── layernorm.py
│   │   ├── rms_layernorm.py
│   │   ├── rope_embedding.py
│   │   ├── geglu.py
│   │   ├── swiglu.py
│   │   ├── flex_attention.py
│   │   └── moe/               # Mixture of Experts kernels
│   ├── registry/              # Model registry
│   │   ├── registry.py
│   │   ├── _llama.py
│   │   ├── _mistral.py
│   │   ├── _qwen.py
│   │   ├── _gemma.py
│   │   ├── _deepseek.py
│   │   ├── _phi.py
│   │   └── REGISTRY.md
│   ├── dataprep/              # Data preparation
│   └── utils/
│       └── hf_hub.py          # Hugging Face Hub utilities
├── tests/                     # Test suite
│   ├── saving/                # Saving/export tests
│   │   ├── test_unsloth_save.py
│   │   ├── language_models/
│   │   ├── vision_models/
│   │   └── text_to_speech_models/
│   └── utils/

├── README.md

```

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS LOCATION

**Primary File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/save.py` (2,700+ lines)

**Key Export Functions:**

1. **unsloth_save_model()** (line 205)
   - Core save function that handles all save methods
   - Parameters: `save_method` = "lora", "merged_16bit", or "merged_4bit"
   - Supports `push_to_hub` for HuggingFace Hub integration
   - Handles LoRA merging, 4bit merging, and state dict creation

2. **unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()** (line 1321)
   - Merges LoRA adapters into model weights
   - Saves as float16 (for GGUF/llama.cpp) or 4bit
   - Method signature: `.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`

3. **unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()** (line 1364)
   - Pushes merged model to HuggingFace Hub
   - Method signature: `.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`

4. **save_to_gguf()** (line 952)
   - Converts model to GGUF format for llama.cpp
   - Parameters: `quantization_method` (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.)
   - Supports multiple quantization methods in one call
   - Uses llama.cpp for conversion

5. **unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()** (line 1768)
   - Saves as GGUF format locally
   - Method signature: `.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method, ...)`

6. **unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()** (line 1951)
   - Pushes GGUF model to HuggingFace Hub
   - Method signature: `.push_to_hub_gguf(repo_id, quantization_method, ...)`

7. **unsloth_save_pretrained_ggml()** (line 2211)
   - Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
   - For use with llama.cpp

8. **unsloth_push_to_hub_ggml()** (line 2143)
   - Pushes GGML format to Hub

9. **unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()** (line 2502)
   - Saves with TorchAO quantization

10. **patch_saving_functions()** (line 2569)
    - Dynamically patches models to add saving methods
    - Creates: `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, etc.

---

### 3. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS (A AND B MATRICES) STORAGE & ACCESS

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Key Functions:**

**get_lora_parameters()** (line 190)
- Returns 5-tuple: `(weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)`
- Accesses LoRA weights via:
  ```python
  lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary indexed by adapter name
  lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
  A = lora_A_linear.weight              # Extract weight tensor
  B = lora_B_linear.weight
  scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]       # LoRA scaling factor (alpha/r)
  ```

**get_lora_parameters_bias()** (line 235)
- Same as above but also returns bias: `(weight, quant_state, A, B, scale, bias)`

**LoRA Layer Structure (from PEFT):**
- Layer with LoRA has attributes:
  - `layer.lora_A[adapter_name]` → Linear layer containing A matrix
  - `layer.lora_B[adapter_name]` → Linear layer containing B matrix
  - `layer.scaling[adapter_name]` → Alpha/rank scaling factor
  - `layer.base_layer` → Original quantized/full-precision layer
  - `layer.merged` → Boolean flag if LoRA is merged
  - `layer.disable_adapters` → Boolean flag if adapters disabled

**Merging LoRA (from save.py line 159):**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    # W = W + A.T @ B.T * scaling
    W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

---

### 4. MAIN MODEL CLASSES & INHERITANCE

**FastLanguageModel** (primary class)
- **File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/models/loader.py` (line 107)
- **Inherits from:** `FastLlamaModel`
- **Static Methods:**
  - `from_pretrained()` - Load model with LoRA
  - `get_peft_model()` - Apply LoRA to model
  - `for_training()` - Setup for training
  - `for_inference()` - Setup for inference

**Model Implementations:**
- `FastLlamaModel` - Base Llama implementation (`llama.py`)
- `FastMistralModel` - Mistral models (`mistral.py`)
- `FastQwen2Model` - Qwen2 models (`qwen2.py`)
- `FastQwen3Model` - Qwen3 models (`qwen3.py`)
- `FastGemmaModel` - Gemma models (`gemma.py`)
- `FastGemma2Model` - Gemma2 models (`gemma2.py`)
- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite models (`granite.py`)
- `FastVisionModel` - Vision models (`vision.py`)
- `FastTextModel` - Text models (`vision.py`)
- `FastModel` - Universal model loader (`loader.py`)

**LoRA Patching (from llama.py line 2834):**
- Unsloth patches specific layers that have LoRA adapters:
  - MLP layers: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj (line 2834)
  - Attention layers: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj (line 2859)
  - Output projection: o_proj (line 2883)
- Each patched layer gets a custom forward function using Unsloth's optimized kernels

---

### 5. LORA-SPECIFIC CODE LOCATIONS

**Fast LoRA Kernels:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

**Custom Autograd Classes:**

1. **LoRA_MLP** (line 28)
   - Forward: computes `G + A_g @ B_g`, `U + A_u @ B_u`, `W + A_w @ B_w`
   - Backward: computes gradients for A, B matrices
   - Handles SwiGLU and GeGLU variants

2. **LoRA_QKV** (line 224)
   - Forward: computes Q, K, V projections with LoRA
   - Backward: computes gradients for QKV projection LoRA weights

3. **apply_lora_qkv()** (line referenced in llama.py)
   - Applies LoRA to attention query, key, value projections

4. **apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()** (line 178)
   - Applies LoRA to MLP with SwiGLU activation

5. **apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()** (line 194)
   - Applies LoRA to MLP with GeGLU activation

**LoRA Weight Computation:**
```python
# From fast_lora.py line 76-79
Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)  # Q = X @ (W_q + A_q @ B_q)
K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)  # K = X @ (W_k + A_k @ B_k)
V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)  # V = X @ (W_v + A_v @ B_v)
```

**Quantization State Handling:**
- `quant_state` attribute on quantized weights (4bit BnB)
- Dequantization happens via `fast_dequantize()` during forward/backward

---

### 6. EXISTING ONNX-RELATED CODE

**Finding:** **NO ONNX-RELATED CODE EXISTS**

Grep search for "ONNX" and "onnx" across all .py files returned zero results. The codebase currently does NOT include any ONNX serialization or export functionality.

---

### 7. SERIALIZATION PATTERNS

**Save Methods Chain (from save.py):**

1. **State Dict Creation** (line 550)
   - `state_dict = OrderedDict()`
   - Embeddings: `state_dict["model.embed_tokens.weight"]`
   - Per-layer: `state_dict[f"model.layers.{j}.{item_name}"]`
   - Manages VRAM vs CPU placement for large models

2. **Merging Pattern** (line 548-600)
   - Iterates through model layers
   - Calls `_merge_lora()` for LoRA layers
   - Dequantizes 4bit weights if needed
   - Accumulates merged weights in state_dict

3. **Saving Methods:**
   - **PEFT Format** (default): Uses `model.save_pretrained()`
   - **Merged Format**: Uses custom state_dict merging
   - **GGUF Format**: Converts to fp16 → HF safetensors → llama.cpp conversion
   - **GGML Format**: Exports LoRA adapters for llama.cpp

4. **Push to Hub:**
   - Wraps HF `push_to_hub()` with metadata tagging
   - Stores "unsloth" tag in model card
   - Commits with "Trained with Unsloth" messages

---

### 8. KEY TEST FILES

**Save/Export Tests:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` - Main save tests
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/tests/saving/language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py` - Push merged tests
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_mistral.py` - Merge quality tests

**Test Pattern:**
```python
model, tokenizer = FastModel.from_pretrained(
    "model_name",
    max_seq_length=128,
    load_in_4bit=True,
)
model = FastModel.get_peft_model(
    model,
    r=16,
    target_modules=["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj"],
    lora_alpha=16,
)
# Then call save methods:
model.save_pretrained_merged("./output")
model.push_to_hub_merged("repo_id")
model.save_pretrained_gguf("./output", quantization_method="q4_k_m")
```

---

### 9. QUANTIZATION & EXPORT SUPPORT

**Supported Quantization Methods for GGUF:**
- `not_quantized` (f16/bf16)
- `fast_quantized` (q8_0)
- `quantized` (q4_k_m)
- `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`, `q3_k_xs`
- `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q4_k`, `q4_k_m`, `q4_k_s`
- `q5_0`, `q5_1`, `q5_k`, `q5_k_m`, `q5_k_s`
- `q6_k`
- `f32`, `f16`, `bf16`

**Export Formats:**
1. **PEFT** - LoRA adapters only
2. **Merged (16bit)** - LoRA merged into float16 weights
3. **Merged (4bit)** - LoRA merged into 4bit quantized weights
4. **GGUF** - Via llama.cpp conversion (supports multiple quantization methods)
5. **GGML** - LoRA in GGML format for llama.cpp
6. **TorchAO** - Experimental support for TorchAO quantization

---

### 10. IMPORTANT INTEGRATION POINTS

**Patching System (from save.py line 2569):**
- `patch_saving_functions(model)` called after model loading
- Dynamically adds methods to model instance:
  - `model.save_pretrained_merged()`
  - `model.push_to_hub_merged()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_gguf()`
  - `model.push_to_hub_gguf()`
  - `model.save_pretrained_ggml()`
  - `model.push_to_hub_ggml()`
- Preserves original `push_to_hub` as `original_push_to_hub`

**Memory Management:**
- Tracks VRAM usage: `torch.cuda.memory_allocated()`
- Configurable maximum memory usage (default 0.75-0.9)
- Saves state to GPU, CPU, or disk based on available memory

---

## Summary

The unsloth codebase is well-organized for model optimization and fine-tuning. It provides:

- **Multiple save/export pathways** (PEFT, merged, GGUF, GGML, TorchAO)
- **Transparent LoRA access** via PEFT's standard `lora_A` and `lora_B` dictionaries
- **Custom high-performance kernels** for LoRA forward/backward passes
- **Comprehensive model support** (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma, etc.)
- **No existing ONNX support** - this is a gap that could be filled

All major save/export logic is centralized in `save.py`, making it the primary file for understanding export mechanisms. The kernel-level LoRA access is abstracted through `get_lora_parameters()` in `kernels/utils.py`.

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT METHODS LOCATION

**Primary File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/save.py` (2,700+ lines)

**Key Export Functions:**

1. **unsloth_save_model()** (line 205)
   - Core save function that handles all save methods
   - Parameters: `save_method` = "lora", "merged_16bit", or "merged_4bit"
   - Supports `push_to_hub` for HuggingFace Hub integration
   - Handles LoRA merging, 4bit merging, and state dict creation

2. **unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()** (line 1321)
   - Merges LoRA adapters into model weights
   - Saves as float16 (for GGUF/llama.cpp) or 4bit
   - Method signature: `.save_pretrained_merged(save_directory, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`

3. **unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()** (line 1364)
   - Pushes merged model to HuggingFace Hub
   - Method signature: `.push_to_hub_merged(repo_id, tokenizer, save_method, ...)`

4. **save_to_gguf()** (line 952)
   - Converts model to GGUF format for llama.cpp
   - Parameters: `quantization_method` (q4_k_m, q5_k_m, q8_0, etc.)
   - Supports multiple quantization methods in one call
   - Uses llama.cpp for conversion

5. **unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()** (line 1768)
   - Saves as GGUF format locally
   - Method signature: `.save_pretrained_gguf(save_directory, quantization_method, ...)`

6. **unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf()** (line 1951)
   - Pushes GGUF model to HuggingFace Hub

7. **unsloth_save_pretrained_ggml()** (line 2211)

8. **unsloth_push_to_hub_ggml()** (line 2143)
   - Pushes GGML format to Hub

9. **unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()** (line 2502)
   - Saves with TorchAO quantization

10. **patch_saving_functions()** (line 2569)
    - Dynamically patches models to add saving methods
    - Creates: `push_to_hub_merged`, `save_pretrained_merged`, `push_to_hub_gguf`, `save_pretrained_gguf`, etc.

---

### 3. LORA ADAPTER WEIGHTS (A AND B MATRICES) STORAGE & ACCESS

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/kernels/utils.py`

**Key Functions:**

**get_lora_parameters()** (line 190)
- Returns 5-tuple: `(weight, quant_state, lora_A, lora_B, lora_scale)`
- Accesses LoRA weights via:
  ```python
  lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary indexed by adapter name
  lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
  A = lora_A_linear.weight              # Extract weight tensor
  B = lora_B_linear.weight
  scaling = proj.scaling[adapter]       # LoRA scaling factor (alpha/r)
  ```

**get_lora_parameters_bias()** (line 235)
- Same as above but also returns bias: `(weight, quant_state, A, B, scale, bias)`

**LoRA Layer Structure (from PEFT):**
- Layer with LoRA has attributes:
  - `layer.lora_A[adapter_name]` → Linear layer containing A matrix
  - `layer.lora_B[adapter_name]` → Linear layer containing B matrix
  - `layer.scaling[adapter_name]` → Alpha/rank scaling factor
  - `layer.base_layer` → Original quantized/full-precision layer
  - `layer.merged` → Boolean flag if LoRA is merged
  - `layer.disable_adapters` → Boolean flag if adapters disabled

**Merging LoRA (from save.py line 159):**
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
    # W = W + A.T @ B.T * scaling
    W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha=s)
    W = W.t().to(dtype)
    return W, bias
```

- `FastGraniteModel` - Granite models (`granite.py`)
- `FastVisionModel` - Vision models (`vision.py`)
- `FastTextModel` - Text models (`vision.py`)
- `FastModel` - Universal model loader (`loader.py`)

**LoRA Patching (from llama.py line 2834):**
- Unsloth patches specific layers that have LoRA adapters:
  - MLP layers: gate_proj, up_proj, down_proj (line 2834)
  - Attention layers: q_proj, k_proj, v_proj (line 2859)
  - Output projection: o_proj (line 2883)
- Each patched layer gets a custom forward function using Unsloth's optimized kernels

### 5. LORA-SPECIFIC CODE LOCATIONS

**Fast LoRA Kernels:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775476428788-1e89uz/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py`

**Custom Autograd Classes:**

1. **LoRA_MLP** (line 28)
   - Forward: computes `G + A_g @ B_g`, `U + A_u @ B_u`, `W + A_w @ B_w`
   - Backward: computes gradients for A, B matrices
   - Handles SwiGLU and GeGLU variants

2. **LoRA_QKV** (line 224)
   - Forward: computes Q, K, V projections with LoRA
   - Backward: computes gradients for QKV projection LoRA weights

3. **apply_lora_qkv()** (line referenced in llama.py)
   - Applies LoRA to attention query, key, value projections

4. **apply_lora_mlp_swiglu()** (line 178)
   - Applies LoRA to MLP with SwiGLU activation

5. **apply_lora_mlp_geglu_exact()** (line 194)
   - Applies LoRA to MLP with GeGLU activation

**LoRA Weight Computation:**
```python
# From fast_lora.py line 76-79
Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)  # Q = X @ (W_q + A_q @ B_q)
K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)  # K = X @ (W_k + A_k @ B_k)
V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)  # V = X @ (W_v + A_v @ B_v)
```

**Quantization State Handling:**
- `quant_state` attribute on quantized weights (4bit BnB)
- Dequantization happens via `fast_dequantize()` during forward/backward

```python
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)

        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
```

### 4. KEY IMPLEMENTATION PATTERNS

#### Pattern A: Instance method that wraps and calls module-level function
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321-1361)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit",
    # ... other params ...
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

#### Pattern B: Instance method that calls specialized save then formats output
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768-1850+)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    # ... other params ...
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        # ... more options ...
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        # ... rest of implementation handles GGUF conversion ...
```

#### Pattern C: Simpler instance method with different behavior
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502-2561)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
```

### 6. RECOMMENDATION FOR ADDING `export_to_onnx`

Based on the patterns found, here's how to add a new `export_to_onnx` method:

**Step 1:** Create the core function in `unsloth/save.py`:
```python
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = False,
    optimize_model: bool = True,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    # ... other HF hub params ...
):
    """Convert and export model to ONNX format"""
    # Similar pattern: collect locals, merge LoRA first, then convert
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    arguments["tokenizer"] = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"] = False  # We'll handle push ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit"  # Must be merged first
    del arguments["self"]
    
    # First merge LoRA
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    
    # Then convert to ONNX
    # ... conversion logic ...
```

**Step 2:** Register it in `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2661-2667):
```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

**Step 3:** Optionally add to `__all__` in save.py if exposing as module-level function.

```python
    "save_to_gguf",
    "patch_saving_functions",
    "create_huggingface_repo",
]
```

**Exposed in:** `/unsloth/__init__.py` (line 263)
```python
from .save import *
```

So module-level functions are: `unsloth_save_model`, `save_to_gguf`, `patch_saving_functions`, `create_huggingface_repo`, `print_quantization_methods`

Instance methods are only available after `patch_saving_functions()` is called on a model.

---

### 4. KEY IMPLEMENTATION PATTERNS

#### Pattern A: Instance method that wraps and calls module-level function
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (line 1321-1361)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    save_method          : str = "merged_16bit",
    # ... other params ...
    maximum_memory_usage : float = 0.75,
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 with as few overhead as possible.

        Choose for `save_method` to be either:
        1. `16bit`: Merge LoRA into float16 weights. Useful for GGUF / llama.cpp.
        2.  `4bit`: Merge LoRA into int4 weights. Useful for DPO / HF inference.
        3.  `lora`: Save LoRA adapters with no merging. Useful for HF inference.
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        logger.warning_once(
            "Unsloth: You're not saving a tokenizer as well?\n"\
            "You can do it separately via `tokenizer.save_pretrained(...)`"
        )
    pass

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    del arguments["self"]
    unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass
```

#### Pattern B: Instance method that calls specialized save then formats output
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (line 1768-1850+)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    quantization_method  : str = "fast_quantized",
    first_conversion     : str = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    # ... other params ...
):
    """
        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.

        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
        # ... more options ...
    """
    if tokenizer is None:
        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")

    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["quantization_method"]
    del arguments["first_conversion"]

    # Fix tokenizer adding an extra BOS token at the front
    fix_bos_token, old_chat_template = fix_tokenizer_bos_token(tokenizer)

    # Non blocking install GGUF first
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
            python_install.wait()
        pass
    else:
        # ... rest of implementation handles GGUF conversion ...
```

#### Pattern C: Simpler instance method with different behavior
Example: `unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (line 2502-2561)

```python
def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
```

---

### 5. METHODS CALLED IN TESTS

From `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`:

```python

# Line 214-218

)
```

---

### 6. RECOMMENDATION FOR ADDING `export_to_onnx`

Based on the patterns found, here's how to add a new `export_to_onnx` method:

**Step 1:** Create the core function in `unsloth/save.py`:
```python
def unsloth_export_to_onnx(
    self,
    save_directory: Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer=None,
    opset_version: int = 14,
    use_external_data_format: bool = False,
    optimize_model: bool = True,
    push_to_hub: bool = False,
    token: Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    # ... other HF hub params ...
):
    """Convert and export model to ONNX format"""
    # Similar pattern: collect locals, merge LoRA first, then convert
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"] = self
    arguments["tokenizer"] = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"] = False  # We'll handle push ourselves
    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit"  # Must be merged first
    del arguments["self"]
    
    # First merge LoRA
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    
    # Then convert to ONNX
    # ... conversion logic ...
```

**Step 2:** Register it in `patch_saving_functions()` (line 2661-2667):
```python
model.export_to_onnx = types.MethodType(unsloth_export_to_onnx, model)
```

**Step 3:** Optionally add to `__all__` in save.py if exposing as module-level function.

---

### Summary of Key Files

- **Main implementation:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2679 lines total)
- **Patching calls:** `/unsloth/models/llama.py` (line 2204) and `/unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 702, 918, 964)
- **Exports:** `/unsloth/__init__.py` (line 263: `from .save import *`)
- **Tests:** `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py`

All save/export methods follow a consistent pattern of creating merged/quantized versions before final conversion/export.

# Copyright 2023-present Daniel Han-Chen & the Unsloth team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import warnings, importlib, sys
from packaging.version import Version
import os, re, subprocess, inspect, functools
import numpy as np

# Fix some issues before importing other packages
from .import_fixes import fix_message_factory_issue
fix_message_factory_issue(); del fix_message_factory_issue;

# Check if modules that need patching are already imported
critical_modules = ['trl', 'transformers', 'peft']
already_imported = [mod for mod in critical_modules if mod in sys.modules]

# This check is critical because Unsloth optimizes these libraries by modifying
# their code at import time. If they're imported first, the original (slower,
# more memory-intensive) implementations will be used instead of Unsloth's
# optimized versions, potentially causing OOM errors or slower training.

if already_imported:
    # stacklevel=2 makes warning point to user's import line rather than this library code,
    # showing them exactly where to fix the import order in their script
    warnings.warn(
        f"WARNING: Unsloth should be imported before {', '.join(already_imported)} "
        f"to ensure all optimizations are applied. Your code may run slower or encounter "
        f"memory issues without these optimizations.\n\n"
        f"Please restructure your imports with 'import unsloth' at the top of your file.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
pass

# Unsloth currently does not work on multi GPU setups - sadly we are a 2 brother team so
# enabling it will require much more work, so we have to prioritize. Please understand!
# We do have a beta version, which you can contact us about!
# Thank you for your understanding and we appreciate it immensely!

# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/1266
os.environ["PROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION"] = "python"

# [TODO] Check why some GPUs don't work
#    "pinned_use_cuda_host_register:True,"\
#    "pinned_num_register_threads:8"

# Log Unsloth is being used
os.environ["UNSLOTH_IS_PRESENT"] = "1"

# Try importing PyTorch and check version
try:
    import torch
except ModuleNotFoundError:
    raise ImportError(
        "Unsloth: Pytorch is not installed. Go to https://pytorch.org/.\n"\
        "We have some installation instructions on our Github page."
    )
except Exception as exception:
    raise exception
pass

@functools.cache
def is_hip():
    return bool(getattr(getattr(torch, "version", None), "hip", None))
pass

@functools.cache
def get_device_type():
    if hasattr(torch, "cuda") and torch.cuda.is_available():
        if is_hip():
            return "hip"
        return "cuda"
    elif hasattr(torch, "xpu") and torch.xpu.is_available():
        return "xpu"
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth currently only works on NVIDIA GPUs and Intel GPUs.")
pass
DEVICE_TYPE : str = get_device_type()

@functools.cache
def get_device_count():
    if DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip"):
        return torch.cuda.device_count()
    elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
        return torch.xpu.device_count()
    else:
        return 1
pass

DEVICE_COUNT : int = get_device_count()

# Reduce VRAM usage by reducing fragmentation
# And optimize pinning of memory
# TODO(billishyahao): need to add hip related optimization...
if (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="0"):
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = \
        "expandable_segments:True,"\
        "roundup_power2_divisions:[32:256,64:128,256:64,>:32]"
elif (DEVICE_TYPE in ("cuda", "hip")) and (os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY", "0")=="1") and \
    ("expandable_segments:True" in os.environ.get("PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF", "")):
    warnings.warn(
        "Unsloth: `UNSLOTH_VLLM_STANDBY` is on, but requires `expandable_segments` to be off.\n"\
        "We will remove `expandable_segments`.",
        stacklevel = 2,
    )
    os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"] = re.sub(
        r"expandable\_segments\:True\,?",
        "",
        os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"],
    )
pass
# We support Pytorch 2
# Fixes https://github.com/unslothai/unsloth/issues/38
torch_version = str(re.match(r"[0-9\.]{3,}", str(torch.__version__)).group(0)).split(".")
major_torch, minor_torch = torch_version[0], torch_version[1]
major_torch, minor_torch = int(major_torch), int(minor_torch)
if (major_torch < 2):
    raise ImportError("Unsloth only supports Pytorch 2 for now. Please update your Pytorch to 2.1.\n"\
                      "We have some installation instructions on our Github page.")
elif (major_torch == 2) and (minor_torch < 2):
    # Disable expandable_segments
    del os.environ["PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF"]
pass

# CCE fails on Torch 2.8 and above
# OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 98304, Hardware limit: 65536. Reducing block sizes or `num_stages`
if (major_torch >= 2 and minor_torch >= 8) or (major_torch > 2):
    os.environ["UNSLOTH_ENABLE_CCE"] = "0"
pass

# Fix other issues
import importlib.util
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.metadata import version as importlib_version
from .import_fixes import fix_xformers_performance_issue
fix_xformers_performance_issue(); del fix_xformers_performance_issue;
from .import_fixes import fix_vllm_aimv2_issue
fix_vllm_aimv2_issue(); del fix_vllm_aimv2_issue;
from .import_fixes import ignore_logger_messages
ignore_logger_messages(); del ignore_logger_messages;

# Torch 2.4 has including_emulation
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    major_version, minor_version = torch.cuda.get_device_capability()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = (major_version >= 8)

    old_is_bf16_supported = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported
    if "including_emulation" in str(inspect.signature(old_is_bf16_supported)):
        def is_bf16_supported(including_emulation = False):
            return old_is_bf16_supported(including_emulation)
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    else:
        def is_bf16_supported(): return SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16
        torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported = is_bf16_supported
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported()
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported() does not have including_emulation
    # set SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 as torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
    SUPPORTS_BFLOAT16 = torch.xpu.is_bf16_supported()
pass

# For Gradio HF Spaces?
# if "SPACE_AUTHOR_NAME" not in os.environ and "SPACE_REPO_NAME" not in os.environ:
import triton
if DEVICE_TYPE == "cuda":
    libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
    if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
        try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
        except: pass
    else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs

    # Try loading bitsandbytes and triton
    import bitsandbytes as bnb
    try:
        cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
        libcuda_dirs()
    except:
        warnings.warn(
            "Unsloth: Running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` to link CUDA."\
        )

        if os.path.exists("/usr/lib64-nvidia"):
            os.system("ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia")
        elif os.path.exists("/usr/local"):
            # Sometimes bitsandbytes cannot be linked properly in Runpod for example
            possible_cudas = subprocess.check_output(["ls", "-al", "/usr/local"]).decode("utf-8").split("\n")
            find_cuda = re.compile(r"[\s](cuda\-[\d\.]{2,})$")
            possible_cudas = [find_cuda.search(x) for x in possible_cudas]
            possible_cudas = [x.group(1) for x in possible_cudas if x is not None]

            # Try linking cuda folder, or everything in local
            if len(possible_cudas) == 0:
                os.system("ldconfig /usr/local/")
            else:
                find_number = re.compile(r"([\d\.]{2,})")
                latest_cuda = np.argsort([float(find_number.search(x).group(1)) for x in possible_cudas])[::-1][0]
                latest_cuda = possible_cudas[latest_cuda]
                os.system(f"ldconfig /usr/local/{latest_cuda}")
        pass

        importlib.reload(bnb)
        importlib.reload(triton)
        try:
            libcuda_dirs = lambda: None
            if Version(triton.__version__) >= Version("3.0.0"):
                try: from triton.backends.nvidia.driver import libcuda_dirs
                except: pass
            else: from triton.common.build import libcuda_dirs
            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32 = bnb.functional.lib.cdequantize_blockwise_fp32
            libcuda_dirs()
        except:
            warnings.warn(
                "Unsloth: CUDA is not linked properly.\n"\
                "Try running `python -m bitsandbytes` then `python -m xformers.info`\n"\
                "We tried running `ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` ourselves, but it didn't work.\n"\
                "You need to run in your terminal `sudo ldconfig /usr/lib64-nvidia` yourself, then import Unsloth.\n"\
                "Also try `sudo ldconfig /usr/local/cuda-xx.x` - find the latest cuda version.\n"\
                "Unsloth will still run for now, but maybe it might crash - let's hope it works!"
            )
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "hip":
    # NO-OP for rocm device
    pass
elif DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu":
    # currently intel xpu will not support bnb, will add support in the future
    # TODO: check triton for intel installed properly.
    pass

# Check for unsloth_zoo
try:
    unsloth_zoo_version = importlib_version("unsloth_zoo")
    if Version(unsloth_zoo_version) < Version("2025.9.6"):
        print(
            "Unsloth: Please update Unsloth and Unsloth-Zoo to the latest version!\n"\
            "Do this via `pip install --upgrade --force-reinstall --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth unsloth_zoo`"
        )
        # if os.environ.get("UNSLOTH_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATES", "0") == "0":
        #     try:
        #         os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo")
        #     except:
        #         try:
        #             os.system("pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps --user unsloth_zoo")
        #         except:
        #             raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please update unsloth_zoo via `pip install --upgrade --no-cache-dir --no-deps unsloth_zoo`")
    import unsloth_zoo
except:
    raise ImportError("Unsloth: Please install unsloth_zoo via `pip install unsloth_zoo`")
pass

from .models import *
from .models import __version__
from .save import *
from .chat_templates import *
from .tokenizer_utils import *
from .trainer import *

# Patch TRL trainers for backwards compatibility
_patch_trl_trainer()

gc.collect()
pass


def not_implemented_save(*args, **kwargs):
    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")
pass


def patch_saving_functions(model, vision = False):
    import inspect
    import types
    from typing import Callable, Optional, Union, List

    # And now re add our saving methods!
    if model.push_to_hub.__name__ == "unsloth_push_to_hub":
        original_push_to_hub = model.original_push_to_hub
    else:
        original_push_to_hub = model.push_to_hub
    pass

    signature = str(inspect.signature(original_push_to_hub)).replace("NoneType", "None")
    signature = signature[1:]
    signature = re.sub("<function save at .+?>", "torch.save", signature)
    docs = original_push_to_hub.__doc__.encode("utf-8").decode("utf-8")

    push_to_hub_text = f'''def unsloth_push_to_hub(self, {signature}:
    """
    {docs}
    """
    arguments = dict(locals())
    del arguments["self"]
    if "tags" in arguments and arguments["tags"] is not None:
        assert(isinstance(arguments["tags"], (list, tuple)))
        arguments["tags"] = list(arguments["tags"]) + ["unsloth",]
    elif "tags" in arguments:
        arguments["tags"] = ["unsloth",]
    elif hasattr(self, "add_model_tags"):
        self.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])

    if "commit_message" in arguments:
        commit_message = arguments["commit_message"]
        if commit_message is not None:
            if not commit_message.endswith(" "): commit_message += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_message:
                commit_message += "(Trained with Unsloth)"
        else:
            commit_message = "Upload model trained with Unsloth"
        arguments["commit_message"] = commit_message

    if "commit_description" in arguments:
        commit_description = arguments["commit_description"]
        if commit_description is not None:
            if not commit_description.endswith(" "): commit_description += " "
            if "Unsloth" not in commit_description:
                commit_description += "(Trained with Unsloth 2x faster)"
        else:
            commit_description = "Upload model trained with Unsloth 2x faster"
        arguments["commit_description"] = commit_description

    # Update model tag
    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        _ = upload_to_huggingface(
            self, arguments["repo_id"], arguments["token"],
            "finetuned", "trl", file_location = None,
            old_username = None, private = arguments["private"],
        )
    pass

    try:
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    except:
        del arguments["tags"]
        self.original_push_to_hub(**arguments)
    pass

    if hasattr(self, "config"):
        print("Saved model to https://huggingface.co/" + arguments["repo_id"])
    pass
    '''
    exec(push_to_hub_text, globals())

    original_model = model
    while True:

        if original_model.push_to_hub.__name__ != "unsloth_push_to_hub":
            original_model.original_push_to_hub = original_model.push_to_hub
            original_model.push_to_hub = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub, original_model)
            if hasattr(original_model, "add_model_tags"):
                original_model.add_model_tags(["unsloth",])
            pass
        pass

        if hasattr(original_model, "model"): original_model = original_model.model
        else: break
    pass

    # Add saving methods to top level model
    if not vision:
        if hasattr(model, "config"):
            # Counteract tokenizers
            model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,            model)
            model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged,        model)
            model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf,                      model)
            model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf,                  model)
            model.save_pretrained_torchao   = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,            model)
            model.push_to_hub_ggml       = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub,  model)
            model.save_pretrained_ggml   = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
        pass
    else:
        # Vision only 1 option
        model.push_to_hub_merged     = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged,     model)
        model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
        model.push_to_hub_gguf       = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_gguf   = types.MethodType(save_to_gguf_generic,                   model)
        model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao,       model)
    pass
    return model
pass

torch_float16  = torch.float16
torch_bfloat16 = torch.bfloat16

def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    """
    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
    """
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
    A = lora_A_linear.weight
    B = lora_B_linear.weight
    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        A,
        B,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
    )
pass


def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
    # For DPO or disabled adapters
    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
    W = base_layer.weight

    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
    pass

    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]

    return (
        W,
        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
        proj.scaling[adapter],
        base_layer.bias,
    )
pass

    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.

def unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(
    self,
    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
    tokenizer            = None,
    torchao_config       = None,
    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
):
    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint

    Args
      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
    """
    # first merge the lora weights
    arguments = dict(locals())
    arguments["model"]        = self
    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
    del arguments["self"]
    del arguments["torchao_config"]
    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
    from torchao import quantize_
    if torchao_config is None:
        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
    else:
        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
        arguments["save_directory"],
        device_map = "auto",
        quantization_config = quantization_config,
        **kwargs,
    )

    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"

    if push_to_hub:
        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
    else:
        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
    pass
    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()
pass

# Corrected function to save LoRA to a custom directory
def save_lora_to_custom_dir(model, tokenizer, save_directory):
    # Create the custom directory if it doesn't exist
    os.makedirs(save_directory, exist_ok=True)

    # Call the unsloth_save_model function with the custom directory
    unsloth_save_model(
        model,
        tokenizer,
        save_directory=save_directory,
        save_method="lora",
        push_to_hub=False,
    )

# Corrected method within the model class to convert LoRA to GGML and push to Hugging Face Hub
def unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub(
    self,
    tokenizer,
    repo_id: str,
    use_temp_dir: Optional[bool] = None,
    commit_message: Optional[str] = "Converted LoRA to GGML with Unsloth",
    private: Optional[bool] = None,
    token: Union[bool, str, None] = None,
    create_pr: bool = False,
    revision: str = None,
    commit_description: str = "Convert LoRA to GGML format using Unsloth",
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):
    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):
        if IS_KAGGLE_ENVIRONMENT:
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            python_install.wait()
            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)
            makefile = None
        else:
            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["protobuf"])
            git_clone.wait()
            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
            python_install.wait()
    else:
        makefile = None

    for _ in range(3):
        gc.collect()

    lora_directory_push = "lora-to-ggml-push"
    save_lora_to_custom_dir(self, tokenizer, lora_directory_push)

    model_type = self.config.model_type
    output_file = os.path.join(lora_directory_push, "ggml-adapter-model.bin")

    print(f"Unsloth: Converting auto-saved LoRA adapters at {lora_directory_push} to GGML format.")
    print(f"The output file will be {output_file}")

    command = f"python3 llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py {lora_directory_push} {output_file} llama"

    try:
        with subprocess.Popen(command, shell=True, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, bufsize=1, universal_newlines=True) as sp:
            for line in sp.stdout:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            for line in sp.stderr:
                print(line, end="", flush=True)
            sp.wait()
            if sp.returncode != 0:
                raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(sp.returncode, command)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print(f"Error: Conversion failed with return code {e.returncode}")
        return

    print(f"Unsloth: Conversion completed! Output file: {output_file}")

    print("Unsloth: Uploading GGML file to Hugging Face Hub...")
    username = upload_to_huggingface(
        self, repo_id, token,
        "GGML converted LoRA", "ggml", output_file, None, private,
    )
    link = f"{repo_id.lstrip('/')}"
    print("Unsloth: Done.")
    print(f"Converted LoRA to GGML and uploaded to https://huggingface.co/{link}")
    print("\nThis GGML making function was made by Maheswar. Ping him @Maheswar on the Unsloth Discord or on HuggingFace (@mahiatlinux) if you like this!")

    tokenizer,
    temporary_location: str = "_unsloth_temporary_saved_buffers",
    maximum_memory_usage: float = 0.85,
):

# Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)

            logger.warning_once(

                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
            )

            delete_strategy.execute()
        pass
